---
title: "Jan Schlegel"
image: profile.jpg
format:
  html:
    css: styles.scss
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@3.5.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: laptop
      text: Blog
      href: https://jhschlegel.github.io
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jan-heinrich-schlegel/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/JHSchlegel
---

::: {.custom-left}
Hi there! I'm Jan, a statistician and product manager based in Zurich. I recently completed my Master's in Statistics at ETH Zurich, specializing in probabilistic AI, high-dimensional statistics, and causality. My work focuses on understanding and improving how machine learning systems make decisions—from interpreting generative models to building robust methods for high-stakes applications like climate forecasting and reinsurance.

When I'm not working on difficult (for me) problems, you'll find me running, in the gym, swimming, building PCs, or getting lost (intentionally) in GeoGuessr.
:::

## Education

::: {.timeline-container}

::: {.education-card}
### Master of Science (M.Sc. ETH) in Statistics
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> ETH Zurich <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Sep 2023 – Sep 2025
:::

::: {.gpa-badge}
GPA: 5.99 / 6.00
:::
- **Focus:** (Probabilistic) Machine Learning, Statistical Learning Theory, Computational Statistics, Causality
- **Thesis:** Mechanistic Interpretability in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models (Grade: 6.00/6.00)
- **Semester Paper:** Extrapolation and Distributional Robustness for Climate Downscaling
:::

::: {.education-card}
### Bachelor of Arts in Business and Economics
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> University of Zurich <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Sep 2019 – Feb 2023
:::

::: {.gpa-badge}
GPA: 5.87 / 6.00 (Class Rank 1)
:::
- **Major:** Banking and Finance; **Minor:** Statistics
- **Thesis:** Portfolio Value at Risk Forecasting with Copula-GARCH Models (Grade: 6.00/6.00)
- Completed 215 ECTS (vs. standard 180) with a strong focus on quantitative finance, statistics, and computing
:::

:::

## Experience

::: {.timeline-container}

::: {.education-card}
### Product Manager
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> Swiss Re <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Feb 2026 – Present
:::
- Drive robust portfolio optimization initiatives across multiple product lines
- Transitioned to full-time role following successful quantitative internship
:::

::: {.education-card}
### Quantitative Intern
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> Swiss Re <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Oct 2025 – Jan 2026
:::
- Developed robust high-dimensional covariance estimation methods applicable to multiple reinsurance contexts
- Designed robust portfolio optimization techniques tailored to reinsurance, providing actionable steering insights
:::

::: {.education-card}
### Biostatistics Research Assistant
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> University of Zurich (EBPI) <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Feb 2022 – Aug 2023
:::
- Contributed to data and code management and reproducible statistical workflows for a COVID-19 cohort study published in *Nature Communications*
- Applied statistical modelling techniques to complex longitudinal datasets and presented the results to principal investigators and the research team
:::

::: {.education-card}
### Accountant (Military Service)
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> Swiss Armed Forces <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Kloten, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Jul 2021 – Nov 2021
:::
- Successfully balanced full-time university studies with mandatory military service obligations
- Managed the complete accounting for a 150-person military unit
:::

::: {.education-card}
### Teaching Assistant Mathematics
::: {.meta-info}
<i class="ri-building-line"></i> University of Zurich <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-map-pin-line"></i> Zurich, Switzerland <span class="meta-sep">·</span> <i class="ri-calendar-line"></i> Sep 2020 – Jul 2021
:::
- Led twice-weekly tutorials covering Analysis and Linear Algebra for groups of up to 30 students
:::

:::
